metadata.title: The Non-Delegation Audit: Four Tests for Tasks That Should Never Go to Claude
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. The most dangerous AI delegation failures happen when you can delegate — but shouldn't.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. The most dangerous AI delegation failures happen when you can delegate — but shouldn't.

beats.narration_text: Four categories of non-delegatable tasks: involves regulated or sensitive data such as HIPAA or legal filings; action is irreversible — sends emails, publishes content, modifies production; requires relationship context only the human has; output cannot be verified without doing the work yourself. Missing any of these is how you discover the category the hard way.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Four categories of non-delegatable tasks: involves regulated or sensitive data such as HIPAA or legal filings; action is irreversible — sends emails, publishes

beats.narration_text: For each of these tasks, determine if delegation is appropriate. Apply four tests: involves regulated or sensitive data, action is irreversible, requires relationship context only the human has, output cannot be verified without doing the work yourself.

props.command: claude "For each of these tasks, determine if delegation is appropriate. Apply four tests: (1) involves regulated/sensitive data, (2) action is irreversible, (3) requires relationship context only the human has, (4) output cannot be verified without doing the work yourself. Return: task, verdict (delegate/do-not-delegate), blocking reason." < tasks.txt

beats.narration_text: The delegation audit script runs tasks through 4 tests and produces a verdict with a blocking reason for each.

Animated decision tree: 5 tasks routed through 4 tests. Two tasks route to DO NOT DELEGATE — one fails the regulated data test, one fails the irreversible action test. Three tasks route to DELEGATE after passing all four tests.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Animated decision tree: 5 tasks routed through 4 tests. Two tasks route to DO NOT DELEGATE — one fails the regulated data test, one fails the irreversible actio

beats.narration_text: Add fifth test: is the output testable against an external ground truth?

props.command: claude "Re-run the audit with a fifth test: is the output testable against an external ground truth? Tasks that cannot be externally verified should not be delegated." < tasks.txt

beats.narration_text: Same tree with fifth test GROUND TRUTH added. One borderline task that previously routed to DELEGATE now fails the fifth test and routes to DO NOT DELEGATE. Blocking reason: no external ground truth to verify synthesis claims against.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Same tree with fifth test GROUND TRUTH added. One borderline task that previously routed to DELEGATE now fails the fifth test and routes to DO NOT DELEGATE. Blo

beats.narration_text: The non-delegation classifier is not about distrust — it's about accountability. You can't hold Claude responsible for a legal filing or a medical recommendation. The four tests exist so you know which category you're in before you discover it through a failure.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The non-delegation classifier is not about distrust — it's about accountability. You can't hold Claude responsible for a legal filing or a medical recommendatio

beats.narration_text: Next: assemble a research packet with source traceability.
